agents.role: Ask-the-factory: a natural-language question over the workshop's history returns a sourced answer.
outputs: a sourced answer in the issue/thread
agents.description: The question is untrusted (fenced); retrieval (tools/ask.py) is over repo history only; every answer carries its sources. read_only.

agents.role: Per-shift operator briefing: last move, the quality number, the top ranked desk items — a <30-second read.
agents.description: Deterministic assembly (tools/briefing.py) from repo data; a live agent may narrate. read_only — posts an issue, writes no repo files.

agents.role: Failed-shift diagnostician: a failure yields a plausible root cause and a next step, opened as an ops-alarm.
agents.description: Deterministic triage (tools/diagnose.py) over run logs + journal tail; reuses the auth classifier. read_only — raises an alarm, writes no repo files.

agents.role: The product loop — designs, builds, tests, reviews, and publishes plugins one iteration at a time (LOOP.md).
agents.description: Dormant under ADR-032. Model work may run only in a live, attended interactive session; GitHub Actions and headless runners cannot invoke it. Session changes still land through reviewed pull requests and required gates.

agents.role: Naming ceremony: proposes plugin-name candidates that exclude existing slugs and obvious collisions, before the name spreads.
outputs: the owner's desk (a name choice with one-tap selection)
agents.description: The company is already named (Nightshift Foundry, ADR-011) — this scopes to plugin names. Collision check (tools/naming.py) is deterministic; names are forever (immutable slugs), so the check runs BEFORE spec locks the name.

agents.role: The chief of staff — collects agent changesets from the outbox, resolves conflicts by precedence, runs guard + gates, lands attributed commits. The single writer (P0.7).
agents.description: Deterministic python — needs no Claude session, so it runs even while the loop is token-paused. Permitted direct writer with foundry-loop (charter/AGENTS.md hard rule 1).

agents.role: Writes a blameless postmortem after an incident: what happened, why it looked fine, the fix, and a runbook delta an operator could follow.
agents.description: Proposes only — postmortems land via the orchestrator after review. 'The AI wrote its own postmortem' is a trust artifact (MASTER P5.4). Blameless: names failure modes, never people or agents-as-culprits. Feeds lessons to tools/memory.py.

agents.role: The state-of-the-company report: real metric movement, honest failures, 3-5 recommendations landed on the desk.
agents.description: Deterministic assembly (tools/quarterly.py) from METRICS.jsonl + records + postmortems; lands recs via the desk API (not a repo changeset). Use as a re-launch (MASTER §11).

agents.role: Commission red-team: an adversarial pass over each commission — planted malicious ones are flagged and held, clean ones pass.
agents.description: On top of the bought guardrails + fence: an adversarial screen (tools/redteam.py) over fenced commission text. Nothing ships that the fenced request didn't legitimately require (patron-text law). Proposes only.

agents.role: Weekly ecosystem scout: dated competitive intel into COMPETITIVE.md, every fetched line fenced as untrusted.
agents.description: Ingests untrusted web text — MUST fence via tools/fence.py before any prompt (hard rule 2), holds NO write capability in the same pass (read/act split). Proposes COMPETITIVE.md updates to the desk.

agents.role: Spec-drift auditor: watches Anthropic's plugin spec; a change surfaces as a desk item citing the doc — no schema edit lands without approval.
agents.description: Fetches Anthropic's live plugin docs (fenced, hard rule 2), diffs vs the encoded schema (tools/specdrift.py). Proposes only — schema changes stay human-ratified (constitution Art. I §5).

agents.role: Steer-by-issue: a one-sentence steering issue becomes a valid backlog item; rule-touching steers become desk items.
agents.description: Ingests untrusted issue text — fenced (hard rule 2). Proposes only; a rule-touching steer never becomes a silent backlog item, it routes to the desk. High-risk steers are held for the red-team, not obeyed.

agents.role: Tripwire auditor: re-audits rubber-stamps — a perfect QA/review streak triggers a fresh adversarial audit.
agents.description: Counters the Project Vend rubber-stamp failure. tools/tripwire.py detects the LOOP.md rule-7 streaks; the agent writes a concrete adversarial re-audit listing attacks attempted. Proposes only.
